parameters.content:
## Meeting Recording to Slack Action Items Bot — WayinVideo + GPT-4o-mini + Slack + Sheets

For teams who run regular standups, project meetings, or client calls and want to automatically share action items with the team on Slack — without anyone manually writing notes or copy-pasting summaries. Paste any Zoom, Google Meet, or Teams recording URL via the form. WayinVideo transcribes the full meeting with speaker labels and timestamps. GPT-4o-mini reads the transcript and extracts five structured sections: a meeting summary, action items with owners and due dates, decisions made, key discussion points, and next steps. A formatted Slack Block Kit message is posted to your team channel automatically. Every meeting summary is also logged to Google Sheets with the Slack message timestamp for thread tracking.

## How it works
- **1. Form — Meeting URL + Details** collects the recording URL, meeting title, team name, date, and Slack channel name
- **2. WayinVideo — Submit Transcription** submits the URL for speaker-labeled transcription
- **3. Wait — 90 Seconds** gives the API initial processing time for meeting-length recordings
- **4. WayinVideo — Get Transcript Results** polls for the task result
- **5. IF — Transcription Complete?** checks for SUCCEEDED — if not, retries via 30-second wait
- **7. Code — Format Transcript** formats each segment with speaker labels and timestamps
- **8. AI Agent — Extract Meeting Summary** uses GPT-4o-mini to extract all five sections in labeled format
- **10. Code — Build Slack Message** parses the five sections via regex and builds a Slack Block Kit JSON message with header, attendee fields, dividers, and a recording link
- **11. Slack — Post Action Items** posts the Block Kit message to your team channel
- **12. Google Sheets — Log Meeting Summary** logs all extracted fields plus the Slack message timestamp

## Set up steps
1. In **2. WayinVideo — Submit Transcription** and **4. WayinVideo — Get Transcript Results** — replace YOUR_WAYINVIDEO_API_KEY
2. In **9. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
3. In **11. Slack — Post Action Items** — connect your Slack OAuth2 credential and replace YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID
4. In **12. Google Sheets — Log Meeting Summary** — connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential and replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID
5. Create a Google Sheet tab named Meeting Log with columns: Meeting Title, Team, Date, Duration (min), Attendees, Summary, Action Items, Decisions, Next Steps, Recording URL, Slack Channel, Slack Message TS, Logged On

## Form Input
User submits meeting recording URL, meeting title, team name, date, and Slack channel name. All fields feed into the transcript formatter and AI prompt.

## WayinVideo Transcription Submit and Poll
Submits the meeting URL for speaker-labeled transcription. Waits 90 seconds for initial processing. Polls the results endpoint until status equals SUCCEEDED.

nodes.name: Section — Status Check and Retry Loop

parameters.content:
## Transcription Status Check and Retry Loop
IF checks for SUCCEEDED status. TRUE proceeds to transcript formatting. FALSE waits 30 seconds and polls again. Loop continues until the transcript is ready.

nodes.name: Section — Transcript Formatting and AI Meeting Extraction

parameters.content:
## Transcript Formatting and AI Meeting Extraction
Formats transcript with speaker labels and timestamps. GPT-4o-mini extracts five structured sections: meeting summary, action items with owners and due dates, decisions made, key discussion points, and next steps.

nodes.name: Section — Slack Message Build, Post, and Sheet Log

parameters.content:
## Slack Message Build, Post, and Sheet Log
Builds a Slack Block Kit message with header, team metadata fields, five content sections, and a recording link. Posts to the team channel. Logs all extracted fields and the Slack message timestamp to Google Sheets.

e.g. Q2 Sprint Planning, Client Kickoff Call, Weekly Standup
e.g. Marketing Team, Engineering, Sales, Product

parameters.formDescription: Paste your meeting recording URL. AI will transcribe the full meeting and automatically post action items, decisions, and key points to your Slack channel.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an expert meeting analyst who extracts clear, actionable summaries from meeting transcripts.

Your job is to read this meeting transcript and extract everything the team needs to know — action items with owners, decisions made, key discussion points, and next steps. The output will be posted directly to Slack so it must be clean, concise, and easy to scan.

---

## MEETING DETAILS
- Meeting Title: {{ $json.meetingTitle }}
- Team: {{ $json.teamName }}
- Date: {{ $json.meetingDate }}
- Duration: {{ $json.totalMinutes }} minutes
- Attendees: {{ $json.speakers }}
- Recording: {{ $json.recordingUrl }}

---

## EXTRACTION RULES
1. Extract ONLY from what was actually discussed in the transcript — no invented content
2. Action items must have an owner name if mentioned — use 'Team' if no specific person assigned
3. Keep each point short — one clear sentence maximum
4. Decisions must be final conclusions reached in the meeting — not suggestions or maybes
5. Key points should be the most important things discussed — max 5 points
6. Next steps are time-bound follow-ups mentioned in the meeting
7. If something is not mentioned in the transcript — leave that section empty with 'None mentioned'

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT
Return your response in this exact structure — use these exact labels:

MEETING SUMMARY:
[Write 2-3 sentences summarizing what the meeting was about and what was accomplished]

ACTION ITEMS:
- [ ] [Action item description] — Owner: [Person name or Team] — Due: [Date if mentioned, else 'TBD']
- [ ] [Action item description] — Owner: [Person name or Team] — Due: [Date if mentioned, else 'TBD']
[Add more as needed — if none, write 'None identified']

DECISIONS MADE:
- [Decision 1 — one clear sentence]
- [Decision 2 — one clear sentence]
[Add more as needed — if none, write 'None recorded']

KEY DISCUSSION POINTS:
- [Point 1]
- [Point 2]
- [Point 3]
[Maximum 5 points — if none, write 'None recorded']

NEXT STEPS:
- [Next step 1 with owner if mentioned]
- [Next step 2 with owner if mentioned]
[Add more as needed — if none, write 'None mentioned']

---

Now read the transcript and extract the meeting summary:
